questions.q: The Union, State and Concurrent Lists are contained in which Schedule of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule sets out the three lists that distribute legislative powers.

questions.q: How many lists divide legislative subjects under the Seventh Schedule?
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule contains three lists: the Union List, the State List and the Concurrent List.

questions.q: Subjects of national importance, such as defence and foreign affairs, fall under which list?
questions.explanation: The Union List covers matters of national importance on which only Parliament can legislate.

questions.q: On which list can both Parliament and the State Legislatures make laws?
questions.explanation: The Concurrent List contains subjects on which both the Union and the States may legislate.

questions.q: Subjects of local or regional importance generally appear in which list?
questions.explanation: The State List covers matters of local or state importance on which state legislatures may legislate.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution gives effect to the distribution of subjects in the three lists?
questions.explanation: Article 246 read with the Seventh Schedule distributes legislative powers among the three lists.

questions.q: Defence of India is a subject under which list?
questions.explanation: Defence is a Union List subject, as it concerns the whole nation.

questions.q: Police and public order are subjects under which list?
questions.explanation: Police and public order are State List subjects, handled primarily by the states.

questions.q: The three lists distribute powers between which two levels of government?
questions.explanation: The lists divide legislative powers between the Union and the States.

questions.q: Foreign affairs and diplomacy are placed under which list?
questions.explanation: Foreign affairs is a Union List subject, reserved for Parliament.

questions.q: In the original Constitution, the Union List contained approximately how many entries?
questions.explanation: The Union List originally contained 97 entries, since modified by various amendments.

questions.q: In the original Constitution, the State List contained approximately how many entries?
questions.explanation: The State List originally contained 66 entries dealing with state and local subjects.

questions.q: In the original Constitution, the Concurrent List contained approximately how many entries?
questions.explanation: The Concurrent List originally contained 47 entries on which both levels could legislate.

questions.q: When a Union law and a State law conflict on a Concurrent List subject, which generally prevails?
questions.explanation: Under Article 254, Union law generally prevails over State law in case of repugnancy on a Concurrent subject.

questions.q: Education, originally a State subject, was moved to which list by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 shifted Education from the State List to the Concurrent List.

questions.q: Which of the following was transferred from the State List to the Concurrent List by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment moved Forests, along with Education and a few others, to the Concurrent List.

questions.q: Atomic energy is a subject under which list?
questions.explanation: Atomic energy is a Union List subject, reserved for the central government.

questions.q: Criminal law and criminal procedure broadly fall under which list?
questions.explanation: Criminal law and criminal procedure are Concurrent List subjects, allowing both levels to legislate.

questions.q: Agriculture is primarily placed under which list?
questions.explanation: Agriculture is a State List subject under the Seventh Schedule.

questions.q: Railways are placed under which list of the Seventh Schedule?
questions.explanation: Railways is a Union List subject, administered by the central government.

questions.q: Which list contains subjects on which only Parliament has exclusive power to legislate?
questions.explanation: The Union List comprises subjects on which only Parliament can make laws.

questions.q: Banking and insurance are placed under which list?
questions.explanation: Banking and insurance are Union List subjects under the Seventh Schedule.

questions.q: Subjects not enumerated in any of the three lists, the residuary subjects, are assigned to whom?
questions.explanation: Article 248 vests residuary powers of legislation, including taxes, in Parliament.

questions.q: Which entry of the Union List acts as the basis for residuary legislative power?
questions.explanation: Entry 97 of the Union List covers any matter not enumerated elsewhere, giving Parliament residuary powers.

questions.q: The principle used to interpret entries broadly and harmoniously when lists appear to overlap is known as:
options: The doctrine of pith and substance
questions.explanation: The doctrine of pith and substance is used to determine the true nature of a law when entries overlap.

questions.q: When a law incidentally encroaches on another list while being in pith and substance within its own list, the doctrine applied is:
options: Colourable legislation, voiding the Union law
questions.explanation: Courts uphold a law that is in pith and substance within the legislature's competence despite incidental encroachment.

questions.q: Weights and measures standards are placed under which list?
questions.explanation: Establishment of standards of weights and measures is a Union List subject.

questions.q: Trade and commerce within a state, and markets and fairs, are placed under which list?
questions.explanation: Intra-state trade and commerce, markets and fairs are State List subjects.

questions.q: A state law on a Concurrent subject that conflicts with a Union law may still prevail in that state if:
options: It has received the President's assent under Article 254(2)
The Governor approves it by order
The state legislature passes it twice
It is published in the Gazette
questions.explanation: Under Article 254(2), a repugnant State law reserved for and assented to by the President prevails in that state.

questions.q: The structure of the three lists in the Seventh Schedule was largely adapted from which earlier statute?
questions.explanation: The three-list scheme was adapted from the distribution of powers under the Government of India Act, 1935.
